subject: Mozi Minute: What Business Are You REALLY In?
body:
Words I like: What got you from zero to a million isn't what's going to get you from 10 to 30 million.

The biggest opportunity is the one right in front of you.

The vast majority of businesses are stuck because they're working on the wrong stuff.

Entrepreneurs get comfortable solving problems they know how to solve, then wonder why they plateau.

Here's why: you're probably in a completely different business than you think.

When I got into gyms, I thought I was in the fitness business. I wasn't. I was in the marketing and sales business.

When I got into supplements, I thought I was in the product business. I wasn't. I was in the brand, media, and distribution business.

When I got into software, I thought I was in the marketing and sales business. I wasn't. I was in the product business.

Real world: Every industry has its "big hairy problem" - the thing that actually determines success.

A gym owner friend started a cleaning business thinking it was like gyms (marketing and sales).

Turns out, cleaning is a recruiting and training business.

Once he flipped his mindset, he went from $40k to $150k in monthly revenue within a year.

How to find your real business:

- Ask successful people in your industry: "What's the hardest part of your business?"

- Look at the biggest companies in your space - what are they exceptional at?

- Identify your constraint - what's keeping you from doubling revenue?

The moment you identify what business you're REALLY in, everything changes.

You stop wasting time on distractions (what I call the "woman in the red dress") and focus on breaking through the concrete wall in front of you.

As promised, money in under a minute.

Solve the right problem, scale the right business.

Alex
